channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Sabedra: California high school basketball season ends with performance for ages
item.description: Riordan coach Joey Curtin on Roosevelt's Brayden Burries: 'He was getting to the rim in transition. Getting to his mid-range. Walking into 3s. That's how you get 44 points on 22 shots.
item.dc:creator: Darren Sabedra, The Mercury News

item.title: DIMES: Why the Oklahoma City Thunder won’t win the NBA championship
item.description: The Thunder look like the title favorite. If they don't lift the Larry O'Brien, it'll be because of these reasons.
item.dc:creator: Danny Emerman, The Mercury News

item.title: Expanding their reach: Even with extra travel here’s why Cal, Stanford say first basketball season in ACC was a success
item.description: Next season most of the ACC’s biggest men's basketball brands – Duke, North Carolina, Louisville and Clemson -- will play at Stanford and Cal.
item.dc:creator: Mike Barber, The Mercury News

item.title: Dodgers' Mookie Betts still struggling with illness, status for opening day in doubt
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Los Angeles Dodgers shortstop Mookie Betts is still struggling to recover from an illness, with manager Dave Roberts pessimistic that the eight-time All-Star will be ready for opening day against the Chicago Cubs on Tuesday in Japan.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BRANDT, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Frustrating start for Lewis Hamilton and Ferrari at F1 Australian Grand Prix
item.description: Lewis Hamilton’s relationship with his new Ferrari team was pressure tested in Australia, where he finished a disappointing 10th on debut, and was frustrated by a bad strategy that cost him a potential victory, and constant radio chatter throughout the difficult race won by McLaren’s Lando Norris.
item.dc:creator: Stewart Bell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: McCallum scores perfect 10 in Red Rocks win over UCLA
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The #5 Red Rocks ended the regular season on a high note, beating #4 UCLA before a packed house at the Huntsman Center Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Real Salt Lake earns 2-1 victory at Houston
item.description: HOUSTON (ABC4 Sports) - Real Salt Lake scored two goals in the first half and successfully held on to defeat the Houston Dynamo 2-1 for a road victory Saturday night. Diogo Goncalves and Diego Luna each punched in goals to lead the offensive attack for RSL (2-2-0, six points).
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: The best team in the National League? For two days in Japan, it's looked like the Hanshin Tigers
item.description: For two days in Japan, it’s the Hanshin Tigers who have looked like the class of the National League.
item.dc:creator: David Brandt, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: McLaren's Lando Norris wins wet and wild Australian Grand Prix. Hamilton finishes 10th
item.description: McLaren’s Lando Norris has won a chaotic rain-affected Australian Grand Prix, his first victory at Albert Park, with the British driver just managing to stay ahead of Red Bull’s Max Verstappen following a third safety car late in the race.
item.dc:creator: Stewart Bell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bickerstaff blasts officials after Pistons receive 5 technical fouls in loss to Thunder
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Pistons coach J.B. Bickerstaff blasted the officials after Detroit was called for five technical fouls in the third quarter of a 113-107 loss to the Oklahoma City Thunder on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about March Madness if you don’t follow college basketball
item.description: No matter how much you know about college basketball — if you’re looking to follow the fun, make your own bracket, or even just understand what all the fuss is about — here are some key things to know.
item.dc:creator: BRIDGET BROWN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jalen Green scores 28 and the Rockets down the Bulls 117-114
item.description: Jalen Green scored 28 points and the Houston Rockets extended their winning streak to six straight games with a 117-114 victory over the Chicago Bulls on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Proctor, Knueppel carry No. 1 Duke past No. 13 Louisville 73-62 for 2nd ACC title in 3 years
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Tyrese Proctor entered Saturday night's ACC championship 0 for 10 from beyond the 3-point arc in his previous two tournament games. That didn't keep him from letting it fly. Proctor made six 3-pointers and scored 19 points, tournament MVP Kon Knueppel added 18 points and No.
item.dc:creator: STEVE REED, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Dick Vitale tears up on ACC Championship broadcast while talking about recent cancer battle
item.description: Dick Vitale became emotional Saturday night while talking about his most recent cancer battle while announcing top-ranked Duke's 73-62 victory over No. 13 Louisville in the Atlantic Coast Conference Tournament championship.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Vikings acquire RB Jordan Mason from 49ers and give him a new 2-year deal, AP source says
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calf. (AP) — The Minnesota Vikings acquired restricted free agent running back Jordan Mason from the San Francisco 49ers and rewarded him with a two-year deal worth up to $12 million.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suns' Bradley Beal still 'smiling every day' amid tumultuous season, but open to offseason trade
item.description: The Phoenix Suns were actively seeking to include Bradley Beal in a trade package last month, but the All-Star guard appears open to moving in the offseason.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Draper knocks off 2-time defending champion Alcaraz to reach Indian Wells final
item.description: INDIAN WELLS, Calif. (AP) — Jack Draper toppled two-time defending champion Carlos Alcaraz 6-1, 0-6, 6-4 on Saturday to reach the BNP Paribas Open final. The 13th-seeded Draper will face 12th-seeded Holger Rune on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No. 6 St. John's beats Creighton 82-66 for 1st Big East Tournament crown in 25 years
item.description: RJ Luis Jr. scored all but two of his 29 points after halftime and No. 6 St. John’s made 14 straight shots from the field in the second half to beat Creighton 82-66 for its first Big East Tournament title in 25 years.
item.dc:creator: Mike Fitzpatrick, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Najee Harris details free agency process, admits he 'just knew' last season was his last with Steelers
item.description: Pro Bowl running back Najee Harris explained why he left the Steelers and signed a contract with the Chargers during the NFL’s free agency period.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: No. 8 Tennessee downs No. 3 Auburn 70-65 to reach SEC Tournament championship game
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Zakai Zeigler scored 20 points as No. 8 Tennessee held off regular-season champion and third-ranked Auburn 70-65 on Saturday to reach the Southeastern Conference Tournament championship for the first time since 2022.
item.dc:creator: TERESA M. WALKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Aaron Rodgers could retire if he doesn't get offer from specific team: report
item.description: Aaron Rodgers may retire if he doesn't get an offer from the Minnesota Vikings. He's weighing offers from the New York Giants and Pittsburgh Steelers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Falcons sticking with Kirk Cousins for now, plan to pay roster bonus: report
item.description: The Atlanta Falcons' front office has decided to keep veteran quarterback Kirk Cousins on the roster through Saturday, which triggers a guaranteed $10 million bonus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UNLV fires coach Kevin Kruger, one of the program's links to its glory-day past
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Coach Kevin Kruger, who led UNLV to the Sweet 16 as the team's point guard in 2007 while playing for his father, Hall of Famer Lon Kruger, was fired by the Rebels on Saturday. Kruger went 76-55 in four seasons and failed to lead the Rebels to the NCAA Tournament.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The 49ers bring back Kyle Juszczyk days after cutting him, AP source says
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — Fullback Kyle Juszczyk is returning to the San Francisco 49ers on a two-year, $8 million contract just days after being cut, a person familiar with the deal told The Associated Press on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rising to the occasion, Shohei Ohtani hits 2-run HR in return to Japan against Yomiuri Giants
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese star Shohei Ohtani showed off some prodigious power in his return to the Tokyo Dome on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BRANDT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Clemson gets locked out of locker room in ACC semifinal; lose narrowly to Louisville
item.description: The Clemson Tigers were forced to gear up for the last 20 minutes of their ACC semifinal game from the halls of Spectrum Center after their locker room was locked.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lewis Hamilton has tough start with Ferrari, qualifying eighth in F1 Australian GP
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Lewis Hamilton’s Ferrari odyssey got off to a tough start on Saturday after the British driver qualified in eighth for Sunday's Australian Grand Prix.
item.dc:creator: STEWART BELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lewis Hamilton 8th in qualifying in Ferrari debut as Norris takes pole for F1 race in Australia
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — McLaren locked up the front row in Melbourne for Sunday’s Australian Grand Prix, having dominated its rivals, including Red Bull, Mercedes, and Ferrari — whose drivers, Charles Leclerc and Lewis Hamilton, will start from seventh and eighth on the grid.
item.dc:creator: STEWART BELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Utah State bounced from MWC Tournament by CSU
item.description: LAS VEGAS (ABC4 Sports) - Utah State cut a 28-point second half deficit down to seven, but ultimately ran out of gas in the Mountain West Conference Tournament semifinals against Colorado State, 83-72. The Aggies (26-7) are still expected to make the NCAA Tournament.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Plucky Lakers nearly beat Nuggets despite Luka Doncic, LeBron James among LA's sidelined starters
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Luka Doncic sat out the Los Angeles Lakers' 131-126 loss at Denver on Friday night, cheering on his scrappy, short-handed teammates who nearly pulled off a shocker without four starters.
item.dc:creator: ARNIE STAPLETON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cougars make 18 three-pointers in 96-92 victory
item.description: KANSAS CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Richie Saunders scored 23 points, hitting a crucial 3-pointer with less than a minute to go, and No. 17 BYU overcame a big game from Curtis Jones in a 96-90 victory over No.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: San Diego FC will take more action against discriminatory fan chants
item.description: San Diego FC has announced additional steps to limit discriminatory behavior by fans, including anti-discriminatory messaging, extra security, and enforcement of sanctions.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, Fox 5

item.title: The Grand Valley's Muay Thai team showed they are not to be messed with
item.description: Out of 36 competing teams from all across the Mountain West, the boys from the Valley finished third.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5
